text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation, status post right cytoreductive nephrectomy and retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. History of disease progression on Opdivo maintenance therapy. Currently on cabozantinib 40 mg daily (dose-reduced from 60 mg for hand-foot skin reaction). Presenting for interval restaging. Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained approximately 3 months ago for comparison.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed in the portal venous phase following administration of 100 mL of nonionic intravenous contrast material (Omnipaque 350). No prior allergic reaction to contrast. Images reconstructed at 2.5 mm axial intervals with coronal and sagittal reformats. Lung windows reviewed for pulmonary parenchyma.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. No endobronchial lesion.

Pulmonary nodules (compared to prior CT from approximately 3 months ago):
- Right lower lobe: The dominant lesion in the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe measures approximately 1.8 cm in longest transaxial dimension (previously 1.9 cm). Essentially stable.
- Left upper lobe: Soft tissue nodule in the left upper lobe, apicoposterior segment, measures 0.9 cm (stable compared to 0.9 cm on prior scan).
- Right upper lobe: Nodule in the right upper lobe anterior segment measures 1.0 cm (stable compared to 1.1 cm).
- Left lower lobe: Nodule in the superior segment of the left lower lobe measures 0.8 cm (previously 0.9 cm), at the margin of measurement reproducibility — essentially unchanged.
- Right middle lobe: Two small nodules in the right middle lobe, measuring 0.4 and 0.5 cm, unchanged from the prior study. These remain subcentimeter.

No new pulmonary nodules identified. No coalescence or cavitation of known nodules. No new consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or tree-in-bud pattern to suggest superimposed infectious or inflammatory process.

Pleura: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No pleural thickening or nodularity.

Mediastinum and hila: The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. No mediastinal or hilar adenopathy by size criteria. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber. No superior vena cava compression.

Chest wall and bony thorax: No suspicious osseous lesions in the visualized ribs, sternum, or thoracic vertebral bodies. Mild degenerative changes in the thoracic spine.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with smooth contour. In the right hepatic lobe, there are two hypodense foci previously described:
- Lesion 1, segment VI/VII: Measures 0.7 cm (previously 0.8 cm). Subtle, but stable in appearance with mild peripheral enhancement on post-contrast imaging.
- Lesion 2, segment VI: Measures 0.5 cm (previously 0.6 cm). Stable and subcentimeter.
No new hepatic lesions identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent.

Right nephrectomy bed: The right kidney is surgically absent. The nephrectomy bed demonstrates expected postoperative changes with fat stranding and surgical clips. No discrete soft tissue mass or nodularity at the operative site to suggest local recurrence. The right adrenal gland appears intact and unremarkable.

Left kidney: The solitary left kidney is mildly enlarged with compensatory hypertrophy. No hydronephrosis. No cortical mass or cyst of concern. Left renal vein patent.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No splenic lesion.

Pancreas: Pancreatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously. No ductal dilatation. No pancreatic mass.

Adrenal glands: Left adrenal gland appears normal. Right adrenal gland preserved and unremarkable as above.

Retroperitoneum and lymph nodes: Surgical clips are present in the retroperitoneum at the prior lymph node dissection site consistent with post-surgical change. No new or enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes identified. Aorta and inferior vena cava patent.

Gastrointestinal tract: Bowel loops appear unremarkable without obstruction or wall thickening. No free air or free fluid.

PELVIS:

Bladder: Normal in appearance. No wall thickening.

Uterus and ovaries: The uterus is small and atrophic consistent with postmenopausal status. No adnexal masses identified.

Pelvic lymph nodes: No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Bony pelvis: No lytic or sclerotic lesions suspicious for osseous metastases. Mild degenerative changes at both sacroiliac joints and bilateral hip joints.

LUMBAR SPINE (visualized): Multilevel degenerative disc disease, most prominent at L3-4 and L4-5. No suspicious vertebral lesions.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. The known bilateral pulmonary nodules are overall stable compared to the prior examination from approximately 3 months ago. The dominant right lower lobe lesion (approximately 1.8 cm) and remaining pulmonary deposits are essentially unchanged in size. No new pulmonary nodules.

2. The two previously identified right hepatic lobe lesions remain small and stable in size (0.7 and 0.5 cm), without convincing growth or new hepatic deposits.

3. No evidence of local recurrence at the right nephrectomy bed.

4. No new osseous lesions or lymphadenopathy.

5. Overall, the pattern is one of disease stability across all sites — pulmonary and hepatic metastatic deposits appear well-controlled without interval growth or new sites of involvement.

Comparing radiologist: [Attending radiologist signature on file]
Dictated and finalized by: Abdominal Imaging, Department of Radiology
